title: Charred Quick Indulgent Moroccan Lamb Tagine
description: Snappy, tender lamb in a rich, coconut-based broth, charred to perfection.
instructions: Season the lamb shoulder with salt, black pepper, and cumin.
Heat the coconut oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the lamb shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes on each side.
Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside.
Add the chopped onion to the pot and cook until fragrant, about 3 minutes.
Add the garlic, ginger, and cumin to the pot and cook for 1 minute.
Add the lamb back to the pot, along with the chicken broth, coconut cream, and spices.
Bring the mixture to a simmer, then cover the pot and transfer it to the preheated oven.
Bake for 30 minutes, then remove the pot from the oven and stir in the chopped fresh parsley.
Return the pot to the oven and bake for an additional 20-25 minutes, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Remove the pot from the oven and let it cool slightly before serving.
Serve the lamb and sauce over couscous, garnished with additional parsley and lemon wedges, if desired.
Ensure poultry is cooked to an internal temperature of 74°C / 165°F (juices run clear, no pink remaining) before serving.
